app.title: Cypress Launcher
tos.title: Welcome to Cypress
tos.desc1: Before you get started, please review and accept our Terms of Service.
tos.desc2: Cypress is a free, open-source community project and is not affiliated with EA or PopCap Games. By using Cypress you agree to our terms regarding data collection, moderation, and usage.
tos.read_full: Read the full Terms of Service
auth.modal_desc: Sign in to link a Cypress identity to be able to join all servers. You can continue without signing in.
auth.username_chars_error: Letters, numbers, spaces, _ and - allowed (no leading/trailing spaces)
auth.refresh_licenses_hint: re-fetches your owned PvZ games from EA
auth.nickname_length_error: Nickname must be 3-32 characters or empty to clear
auth.display_name_hint: your display name when joining servers
identity.confirm_desc1: You are registering with this EA account:
identity.username_desc: Pick a unique username for your Cypress identity. This is how other players will know you.
relink.title: Relink EA Account
relink.warning1: This is a one-time operation. Once you relink, you cannot do it again.
relink.warning2: Make sure you are signing into the correct EA account, and that it owns at least one PvZ shooter (Garden Warfare 1, Garden Warfare 2, or Battle for Neighborville).
relink.btn: Sign in with new EA account
profile.title: Profile
profile.settings_apply: these settings apply to every game
profile.sign_in_prompt: Sign in with your EA account to link a Cypress identity
profile.display_name_hint: your display name when joining servers
profile.nickname_hint: optional display name shown to other players
profile.refresh_licenses_hint: re-fetches your owned PvZ games from EA
profile.relink_ea_hint: link a different EA account (one-time only)
profile.sign_out_hint: signs out of your EA account
profile.library_sign_in: Sign in to see your owned games
profile.dark_mode_desc: Switch to a dark, low-contrast theme
profile.language_hint: Restart not required. Custom translations can be placed in %AppData%\Cypress\Translations.
join.title: Join a Server
join.desc: Connect to an existing Cypress server
join.no_servers: No servers saved. Click Add Server to get started.
join.server_address_placeholder: Select a server above, or enter an address
join.server_address_hint: Use the host's direct IP or VPN address
join.connection_hint: Use Direct for LAN, public IP, or VPN tools like Radmin/Hamachi. Use Relay for a dedicated Cypress relay.
join.relay_code_hint: Enter the 6-character code or paste the full join link the host gave you.
join.relay_region_hint: Pick the same region as the host. Defaults to NA.
join.relay_host_hint: Only needed if the host runs a custom relay server.
server_modal.name_hint: A label for this entry in your list
browser.title: Server Browser
browser.no_servers_hint: Check back later or host your own!
browser.vpn_desc: This server requires a VPN connection. Install and connect before joining.
browser.ban_prompt_body:
Ban server "{name}"?
Enter reason (or leave blank):
host.title: Host a Server
host.desc: Configure and launch a dedicated Cypress server
host.bind_address_hint: Auto-detected for you. Only change this if you want to bind to a different adapter, such as a VPN.
host.relay_hint: Tunnels traffic through the selected relay so you don't need to port-forward. A relay lease is generated automatically when you start the server.
host.relay_share_hint: Share this link with players so they can join your server.
host.listed_browser_hint: When enabled, your server will appear in the public Cypress server browser.
host.block_id_hint: Reject players whose name starts with ID_ (commonly used to cover the screen with long text).
host.motd_hint: Message of the day - supports [b]bold[/b], [i]italic[/i], [c=color]colors[/c], [g=#A,#B]gradients[/g], and animations.
host.server_icon_hint: Optional 64x64 icon shown in the server preview. Large images are automatically resized.
host.modpack_url_hint: If your server uses mods, provide a download link so players can grab the modpack.
host.vpn_server_hint: Enable if players need a VPN to connect. They'll see VPN details before joining.
host.decentralized_hint: Runs the server without any connection to Cypress infrastructure. No server browser listing, no global bans, no account requirements, no global moderators. Ideal for fully private or self-hosted setups.
host.global_ban_db_hint: Cross-references joining players with the Cypress community ban list. Protects your server from known cheaters automatically. Disabling this leaves ban enforcement entirely in your hands, you take full responsibility.
host.require_cypress_hint: Only allows players with a verified Cypress account to join. Enables identity tracking and ban evasion prevention. Disabling this lets anyone join anonymously, you take full responsibility for moderation.
host.allow_global_mods_hint: Permits Cypress global moderators to join and help moderate your server. They can kick or ban disruptive players, and have full administrative privileges on your server. Disabling this means no external moderators can act on your server, you are solely responsible for maintaining order.
mapmode.server_args_placeholder: Toggle modifiers below to generate args
mapmode.variant_hint: Selects which map variation loads for this level.
mapmode.night_unsupported_title: Night may not be fully supported on this map
mapmode.mode_incompatible: "{mode}" is not supported on "{level}". This will likely result in a black screen.
mapmode.night_not_supported_gw1: Night TOD is not supported on "{level}". Use Day instead.
mapmode.night_partial_gw2: Night may not fully work on "{level}". It works best on Seeds of Time and Great White North.
mapmode.start_not_supported: "{sp}" start point is not supported on "{level}".
playlist.title: Playlist Builder
playlist.desc: Visually create playlist JSON files for your server
playlist.mixed_tod_label: Time of Day Per Level (JSON, optional)
docs.title: How To & Reference
instances.title: Instances
instances.launch_hint: Launch a game from Join or Host to see it here
moderator.title: Moderator
moderator.sign_in_desc: Sign in with your EA account to claim moderator access. Your EA account must be on the moderator whitelist.
relay.status_auto_relay: The launcher will use the relay host automatically.
relay.join_address_hint: Use the host's LAN IP, public IP, or VPN address.
relay.bind_address_relay_hint: Usually correct as-is. The relay handles public reachability.
relay.bind_address_direct_hint: Auto-detected for you. Change it only if you want to bind to a different adapter, including a VPN.
relay.preset_custom_hint: Point this to your UDP relay host. Hostnames and host:port are supported.
relay.preset_template_hint: Template only. Replace it with your VPS host or domain before launching.
app.subtitle: Launcher
modifiers.char_restrictions_desc: Restrict which characters can be used
modifiers.raw_costume_desc: Still editable manually if you want to paste a custom AvailableCostumes string.
modifiers.restrict_classes_desc: Restrict which classes can be used
modifiers.class_kill_switches_desc: Prevent players from selecting these classes
modifiers.ai_spawn_restrictions_desc: Disable specific AI classes from spawning
docs.qs_step1_text: EA Desktop must be running with an account that owns the game, or you'll get a black screen on launch.
docs.qs_step2_text: Use the Game dropdown in the sidebar to pick GW1, GW2, or BFN.
docs.qs_step3_text: Click Browse or Auto-detect in the sidebar to locate your game installation folder.
docs.qs_step4_text: Go to the Profile tab and sign in with your EA account. Register a Cypress username if you haven't already - this is your identity on the server browser and in-game.
docs.qs_step5_text:
Joining? The easiest way is the Server Browser tab - just double-click a server. You can also join by IP in the Join tab, or enter a Relay Code if the host gave you one.
Hosting? Configure your map, mode, and settings in the Host tab. Enable EU Relay if you can't port forward.
docs.qs_step6_text: The launcher manages the game process. Closing it will kill the server or disconnect you.
docs.join_browser_step2: Filter by game, player count, or search by name
docs.join_browser_step3: Double-click a server to join. If it has a password, you'll be prompted for it
docs.join_direct_step1: In the Join tab, make sure Connection Path is set to Direct/VPN
docs.join_direct_step2: Enter the host's IP address (public IP, LAN IP, or VPN IP like Radmin/Hamachi)
docs.join_direct_step3: Enter the Server Password if one was set
docs.join_relay_intro: If the host is using the EU Relay (or a custom relay), they'll have a 6-character code to share.
docs.join_relay_step1: In the Join tab, switch Connection Path to Relay
docs.join_relay_step2: Enter the Relay Code the host gave you - the address auto-fills when valid
docs.join_vpn_intro: Only needed if the host is using a VPN instead of port forwarding or relay.
docs.join_vpn_step1: Install Radmin VPN (or similar) and join the host's network
docs.join_vpn_step2: Use the host's VPN IP as the server address
docs.host_relay_intro: The easiest way to host. Your server connects through a relay so players don't need your IP and you don't need to touch your router.
docs.host_relay_step1: In the Host > Network tab, enable EU Relay
docs.host_relay_step2: Configure your map, mode, and any modifiers
docs.host_relay_step3: Click Start Server - you'll get a Relay Code to share with players
docs.host_relay_step4: Your server will also appear in the Server Browser if "Listed in Browser" is enabled
docs.host_portfwd_step1: Open CMD and run ipconfig. Note your IPv4 address and Default Gateway
docs.host_portfwd_step2: Open the Default Gateway IP in your browser and log in to your router
docs.host_portfwd_step3:
Find Port Forwarding settings and create two rules:
• Port 25200, Protocol UDP, IP = your IPv4 (game traffic)
• Port 14638, Protocol TCP, IP = your IPv4 (side channel - used for server browser, identity, and player queries)
For multiple servers, use ranges starting at those ports
docs.host_portfwd_step4: In Host > Network, enter your IPv4 as the Bind Address and click Start Server
docs.host_portfwd_step5: Share your public IP (ipchicken.com) with players
docs.host_portfwd_note: Your IPv4 can change periodically. Re-check with ipconfig if your server stops working. Allow any firewall prompts.
docs.host_radmin_intro: Everyone who wants to join must also install Radmin and join your network.
docs.host_radmin_step1: Install Radmin VPN and create a network
docs.host_radmin_step2: Copy your Radmin IP (shown at top) and enter it as the Bind Address
docs.host_radmin_step4: Share your Radmin network name, password, and IP with players
docs.host_setting_listed: Makes your server visible in the Server Browser tab for everyone
docs.host_setting_gcbdb: Global ban database - auto-kicks known cheaters on join
docs.host_setting_motd: Message of the day shown in the server browser. Supports bold, italic, colors, gradients, and animations
docs.host_setting_icon: Custom 64x64 icon shown in the server browser
docs.host_setting_anticheat: Server-side cheat prevention with configurable checks (Host > Anticheat tab)
docs.commands_intro: Type these into the server window's text input at the bottom (below the FPS/Level info).
docs.commands_runtime_text: You can also change any game setting live by typing it directly, e.g. GameMode.ModeTeamId 2 or GameMode.CrazyOption2 true
docs.cmd_loadnextplaylist: Advance to the next playlist entry
docs.cmd_say: Broadcast a message to all players for N seconds
docs.cmd_saytoplayer: Message a specific player for N seconds
docs.levels_intro: Complete reference of all levels, supported game modes, and how inclusion strings work for each game.
docs.levels_gw1_format_text: GW1 requires the full level path. The number after the GameMode (0 or 1) determines which map variant loads.
docs.levels_gw2_format_text: GW2 uses short Level IDs (not full paths). Inclusion must include HostedMode.
docs.levels_bfn_format_text: BFN uses DSubs (sub-levels of Level_Picnic_Root) and StartPoints which function like sub-gamemodes.
docs.modifiers_intro: All modifiers available per game. These are automatically handled as toggles/dropdowns in the Host > Game Modifiers section. For manual use in launch args, prefix with - and separate with spaces.
docs.mod_gw2_costumes_text: Restrict which characters can be used. Use semicolon-separated character IDs.
docs.mod_bfn_kills_text: Prevent specific classes from being selected using SyncedPVZSettings.CharacterKillSwitches. Format: ["RoseClass","ImpClass",...]
docs.mod_bfn_ai_mask_text: Use SoloPlaySettings.DisabledPlantAIClassMask / DisabledZombieAIClassMask to prevent AI classes from spawning. Add up the values of classes you want to disable.
docs.playlists_setup_text: Create a Playlists folder in your game's directory. Any .json file in that folder will be detected by the launcher.
docs.playlists_tip_text: If you enable settings like GameMode.CrazyOption8 true on one entry, add GameMode.CrazyOption8 false to the next entry's SettingsToApply to turn it off.
docs.pl_rounds_desc: How many rounds of the same setup before rotating
docs.pl_ismixed_desc: If true, levels and modes are randomized instead of following order
docs.pl_ls_gamemode_desc: Override the mode name on load screen (all entries)
docs.pl_ls_level_desc: Override the level name on load screen (all entries)
docs.pl_ls_asset_desc: Override the loading screen image (all entries)
docs.pl_levelname_desc: Full level path: Levels/Level_Rush_Snow/Level_Rush_Snow (GW1 uses its own paths)
docs.pl_loadscreen_desc: Per-entry loadscreen overrides (GamemodeName, LevelName, LevelDescription, UIAssetPath)
docs.pl_avail_modes_desc: Array of mode IDs that will be randomly picked
docs.pl_avail_levels_desc: Object mapping each mode to an array of level paths
docs.pl_avail_tod_desc: (Optional) Object mapping level paths to [Day, Night] arrays
